---
title: "cnet_plot_transcriptomics"
date created: 6/13/25
output: html_notebook
---

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
#  cnetplot_refinement.R – Companion script for Pygo_gene_enrichment workflow
#  Author : Galen O'Shea‑Stone  |   (June 2025)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
#  Purpose :
#    •  Read enrichment result objects (ego, ekegg, ereact) + fold‑change vector
#       saved by the main pipeline (see note below).
#    •  Generate publication‑quality, **refined** 2‑D cnetplots with improved
#       colouring, layout and interactive HTML twins.
#    •  Optionally render an **interactive 3‑D** network using the threejs
#       package (works in any modern browser via WebGL).
#
#  Usage   :
#    Rscript cnetplot_refinement.R  [path/to/enrichment_objects.rds]
#
#  Main‑pipeline hook :
#    Add the following at the very end of your original R Markdown (after
#    the enrichment objects exist):
#
#        saveRDS(list(ego = ego,
#                     ekegg = ekegg,
#                     ereact = ereact,
#                     fc_vec = fc_vec),
#                "output/enrichment_objects.rds")
# 
#   MAKE SURE THAT THE ABOVE FILE IS IN THE WORKING DIRECTORY BEFORE YOU RUN THIS
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------


```{r}

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(clusterProfiler)   # cnetplot()
  library(enrichplot)        # plotting helpers
  library(igraph)            # network backbone
  library(ggraph)            # tidygraph visualisation (2‑D)
  library(ggplot2)           # theming + export
  library(plotly)            # ggplot‑>HTML widget
  library(threejs)           # 3‑D WebGL networks
  library(htmlwidgets)       # saveWidget()
  library(dplyr); library(tidyr); library(glue)
})

# ---- 1.  Input & housekeeping --------------------------------------------- #
args     <- commandArgs(trailingOnly = TRUE)
rds_file <- if (length(args) >= 1) args[1] else "enrichment_objects.rds"
if (!file.exists(rds_file))
  stop("RDS file not found: ", rds_file, call. = FALSE)

objs   <- readRDS(rds_file)
ego    <- objs$ego
ekegg  <- objs$ekegg
ereact <- objs$ereact
fc_vec <- objs$fc_vec   # named numeric: ENTREZID → log2FC

outdir <- "output"
dir.create(outdir, showWarnings = FALSE)

# ---- 2.  Helpers ----------------------------------------------------------- #
refined_cnet <- function(res, fc, showCat = 15, prefix,
                         layout_alg = "kk", wrap_width = 50,
                         gene_label_size = 3, cat_label_size = 4) {
  if (is.null(res) || nrow(as.data.frame(res)) == 0) return(invisible(NULL))
  message("→ Building refined cnetplot for ", prefix)

  # keep top categories by FDR
  res2            <- res
  res2@result     <- res@result %>% arrange(p.adjust) %>% slice_head(n = showCat)

  p <- cnetplot(res2,
                showCategory = showCat,
                foldChange   = fc,
                layout       = layout_alg,   # Kamada‑Kawai – less overlap
                circular     = FALSE,
                node_label   = "all",
                cex_label_category = cat_label_size,
                cex_label_gene     = gene_label_size) +
       scale_color_gradient2(low = "royalblue", mid = "grey90",
                             high = "firebrick", name = "log₂FC") +
       labs(title    = glue("{prefix}: refined cnetplot"),
            subtitle = glue("Top {showCat} categories"))

  ggsave(file.path(outdir, glue("cnetplot_refined_{prefix}.pdf")),
         plot = p, width = 10, height = 8)

  # Interactive HTML twin ----------------------------------------------------
  html_int <- plotly::ggplotly(p, tooltip = "text")
  saveWidget(html_int,
             file.path(outdir, glue("cnetplot_refined_{prefix}.html")),
             selfcontained = TRUE)
  invisible(p)
}

make_cnet_3d <- function(res, fc, showCat = 12, prefix) {
  if (is.null(res) || nrow(as.data.frame(res)) == 0) return(invisible(NULL))
  message("→ Building 3‑D cnetplot for ", prefix)
  df <- as.data.frame(res) %>% arrange(p.adjust) %>% slice_head(n = showCat)

  # Edge list: category ↔ gene ------------------------------------------------
  edges <- df %>%
    select(ID, geneID) %>%
    separate_rows(geneID, sep = "/") %>%
    rename(from = ID, to = geneID)

  # Node table ---------------------------------------------------------------
  genes <- unique(edges$to)
  cats  <- unique(edges$from)
  nodes <- data.frame(name  = c(cats, genes),
                      type  = c(rep("Category", length(cats)),
                                rep("Gene", length(genes))),
                      stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  nodes$color <- ifelse(nodes$type == "Category", "#E74C3C", "#3498DB")

  # Gene‑specific logFC shading (optional) -----------------------------------
  if (!is.null(fc) && length(fc) > 0) {
    fc_lut <- fc[nodes$name]
    rng    <- max(abs(fc_lut), na.rm = TRUE)
    palette_fc <- colorRampPalette(c("royalblue", "white", "firebrick"))(201)
    grads  <- cut(fc_lut + rng, breaks = 200, labels = FALSE)
    nodes$color[nodes$type == "Gene" & !is.na(grads)] <- palette_fc[grads]
  }

  g <- igraph::graph_from_data_frame(edges, directed = FALSE, vertices = nodes)
  w <- threejs::graphjs(g,
                        vertex.color = V(g)$color,
                        vertex.size  = ifelse(V(g)$type == "Category", 1.2, 0.8),
                        main = glue("{prefix}: 3‑D cnetplot"))
  saveWidget(w,
             file.path(outdir, glue("cnetplot_refined_{prefix}_3D.html")),
             selfcontained = TRUE)
  invisible(w)
}

# ---- 3.  Run --------------------------------------------------------------- #
refined_cnet(ego,    fc_vec, 15, "GO_ORA")
refined_cnet(ekegg,  fc_vec, 15, "KEGG_ORA")
refined_cnet(ereact, fc_vec, 15, "Reactome_ORA")

# Comment out any of the following if WebGL is an issue ----------------------
#none of these work as of 6/17/25

#make_cnet_3d(ego,    fc_vec, 10, "GO_ORA")
#make_cnet_3d(ekegg,  fc_vec, 10, "KEGG_ORA")
#make_cnet_3d(ereact, fc_vec, 10, "Reactome_ORA")

message("\n✓ cnetplot refinement complete – see 'output/' for PDFs & HTML widgets\n")


```
